D8 AnthropologyL2 UnderstandReference

Prussian Blue — Accidental Synthesis (1706)

Berlin colour-maker Diesbach contaminated red lake pigment with potash that contained animal blood; ferric ferrocyanide came out, the first stable modern synthetic blue

Fe⁴[Fe(CN)₆]₃ is an intervalence charge-transfer compound — Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺ swap an electron through cyanide bridges. The transition lies near 700 nm; the complement is deep blue. Hokusai used it for "The Great Wave"; Picasso for the Blue Period; it is still in the FDA cyanide-antidote stockpile.

Slide pigment quantity — Beer-Lambert darkening from sky-blue to ink.
